_comment: PJRC Teensy USB VID:PID table, manually curated 2026-06-21.
_provenance: VID 0x16C0 sub-allocated from VOTI to PJRC for the Teensy family.
VID 0x1FC9 owned by NXP Semiconductors (IMXRT family ROM bootloader).
USB-mode PIDs (0x16C0:0x048X / 0x04DX / 0x0476) sourced verbatim from PaulStoffregen/cores/teensy4/usb_desc.h (table is byte-identical in teensy3/usb_desc.h).

HalfKay + rebootor PIDs (0x16C0:0x0478 / 0x0477) sourced from PaulStoffregen/teensy_loader_cli/teensy_loader_cli.c (hardcoded across every per-OS code branch, lines 290/324/337/476/511/669/707/787/814).

NXP IMXRT serial-download PID (0x1FC9:0x0135 for MIMXRT1062, i.e. Teensy 4.0/4.1) cross-confirmed by: adafruit/tinyuf2/ports/mimxrt10xx/Makefile (SDP_MIMXRT1062_PID=0x0135), phoenix-rtos/phoenix-rtos-hostutils/psu/imxrt-flash.sdp (WAIT 0x1fc9 0x0135), molejar/pyMBoot/mboot/connection/usb.py ('IMXRT': (0x1FC9, 0x0135)).
PJRC udev rule (https://www.pjrc.com/teensy/00-teensy.rules) confirms VID 0x1FC9 + PID 013* range with a wildcard.

All Teensy variants (3.0/3.1/3.2/3.5/3.6/LC/4.0/4.1) share the SAME runtime PID table — the PID is determined by the USB-type Arduino-IDE config, NOT by the chip. To disambiguate which Teensy a user has, read the USB Product String.
_skip: ignored by extract_other.py (only records with vid+pid+vendor/product fields are ingested)

product: Teensy 4.x (NXP IMXRT1062 ROM bootloader, serial-download mode)
